text: Film and Media studies visual culture through film, television, and digital media, combining theory, criticism, and hands-on production.
Fine Art (Visual Art) offers intensive studio-based training in painting, sculpture, printmaking, and new media. Students benefit from small cohorts, visiting artists, and experiential learning.

The Ontario Post-Graduate Certificate in First Nations, Métis, and Inuit Studies in Education enhances educators’ expertise in Indigenous education. The program consists of five AQ/ABQ courses accredited by the Ontario College of Teachers and reflects Ontario Ministry of Education policy, OCT regulations, and research-driven educational theory.

metadata.program_name: First Nations, Métis, and Inuit Studies in Education

text: French Studies offers students opportunities to study French language, literature, and culture at varying levels, from beginner to advanced. The program supports minors, majors, and certificates of competency and includes exchange opportunities in Quebec and France.

Gender Studies is an interdisciplinary program examining equity, justice, and gender across societies. Students integrate methods from social sciences, humanities, arts, and natural sciences to produce innovative scholarship and social analysis.

The Geographic Information Science Certificate focuses on geospatial data collection, processing, and analysis. The program enhances technical skills in GIS for students across geography and related disciplines.
Geography integrates social sciences, humanities, and physical sciences to study natural processes, human-environment interactions, and global challenges such as climate change and urban planning.

Geological Engineering combines engineering fundamentals with geological sciences. Students develop expertise in earth processes, geotechnical engineering, field methods, laboratory analysis, and computational modeling.

Geological Sciences focuses on understanding Earth systems through geophysics, geochemistry, geobiology, and field geology. The program supports careers in resource management, environmental protection, infrastructure planning, and climate adaptation.

German Studies develops proficiency in the German language while exploring German culture, history, philosophy, and scientific contributions. Knowledge of German supports careers in international business, research, and cultural fields.

The Global Action and Engagement certificate is an online program focused on global development issues. It prepares students for international, community-based, and volunteer work through interdisciplinary study of development theory and practice.

Global Development Studies empowers students to address global challenges through interdisciplinary analysis and applied problem-solving. Graduates gain specialized knowledge and practical skills for careers in development, policy, and international organizations.

The Bachelor of Health Sciences balances biomedical and social science perspectives to prepare students for diverse health-related careers. Courses emphasize applied learning, real-world scenarios, and practical competencies.

Health Studies examines physical, mental, and social well-being through social science frameworks. Students study health policy, epidemiology, health promotion, and social determinants of health with a strong emphasis on social justice.

Queen’s Concurrent Education is a five-year plus one summer program allowing students to earn an Honours BA, BSc, or Music degree alongside a Bachelor of Education. Graduates are certified by the Ontario College of Teachers and complete their degrees faster than traditional education pathways.

The Certificate in Data Analytics focuses on understanding complex systems through computational modeling and data-driven decision making. Students learn to analyze and interpret data to support informed decision-making across disciplines.

The Certificate in Disability and Physical Activity (DIPA) is offered through Kinesiology and Health Studies in collaboration with Revved Up. It focuses on inclusive, community-based physical activity programs supporting individuals with disabilities or chronic conditions.

The Drama program offers training in acting, directing, design, playwriting, technical theatre, and performance studies. Students develop communication, leadership, and collaborative skills through extensive production-based learning.

Earth Systems Science combines environmental studies with scientific training in physical geography. Students examine interactions between land, water, and air to understand environmental change from a systems perspective.

Economics analyzes how individuals, markets, and governments allocate resources and make decisions. Queen’s Economics programs are known for rigorous training in theory, policy analysis, and applied economics.
The Workplace Training and Development Post-Graduate Certificate prepares professionals to design and deliver effective adult education and training programs outside traditional K–12 settings.

World Language Studies explores the role of multilingualism in a globalized world. The program emphasizes language acquisition, cultural understanding, and global perspectives to prepare students for international careers.
The English program develops critical reading, analytical writing, and communication skills. Students explore literature across historical periods, genres, and critical approaches.

The Mohawk Language and Culture Certificate offers culturally grounded learning experiences focused on Mohawk language, traditions, history, and worldview. The program supports Indigenous language revitalization and cultural education.

The Music program provides comprehensive education in performance, theory, composition, ethnomusicology, and music education. Students benefit from state-of-the-art performance spaces and interdisciplinary opportunities.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science; DAN School of Drama & Music

text: The Music Theatre program combines academic study with intensive practical training in acting, singing, and dancing. Students complete industry-focused coursework and performance training across partner institutions.

metadata.faculty: Arts and Science; DAN School of Drama & Music
